description: Official website for Magic ToDo, an app recommended by the Dyspraxia Foundation to break down complex tasks (like cooking or bed-making) into simple steps. Available for iOS and Android.
title: Magic ToDo

description: Official Google Play Store listing for Magic ToDo, a neurodivergent-friendly task manager that helps break down complex routines into manageable steps for users with dyspraxia.

description: Official Google Play Store listing for Tiimo, a visual daily planner for neurodiverse people. Features include visual routines, time notifications, and activity icons for iOS and Android.

description: Dedicated mobile app designed for dyspraxia support, featuring simple to-do lists, visual reminders, and customizable alerts for daily tasks.
title: Toppa - To-Do List & Reminders for Dyspraxia

description: Official documentation for Google Calendar's reminder system, widely used by neurodivergent users for setting visual alerts and scheduling tasks.

description: Official Microsoft task management app with reminder features, allowing users to create lists, set due dates, and receive notifications for dyspraxia support.
title: Microsoft To Do

description: An interactive iOS and Android app helping children calm down through breathing techniques and mindfulness, featuring Sesame Street's friendly monster to guide them through calming strategies for frustration and distress.
title: Breathe, Think, Do with Sesame

description: A top-rated iOS and Android app for mindfulness, meditation, and sleep, including a dedicated 'Calm Kids' section with relaxation activities and sleep stories designed to help children with anxiety and calming needs.
title: Calm - Mindfulness and Sleep

description: An iOS and Android app structured around five themes (Calm, Focus, Kindness, Sleep, Wake Up) with programs for children aged 512, using breathing exercises and visualizations to support emotional regulation and calming.
title: Headspace for Kids

description: An iOS app designed as a visual and audio tool for children with autism or anxiety, offering a simple countdown mechanism from 10 to help them calm down when angry or anxious, featuring color-changing feedback.
title: Calm Counter

description: A free iOS and Android self-regulation app helping children identify and communicate bodily sensations and emotions, supporting calming strategies for emotional regulation in a healthy way.
title: Trigger Stop: Sensory and Emotional Check-In

description: A calming iOS and Android app encouraging emotion regulation through interactive breathing exercises, using visual bubbles to guide users in slow, mindful breathing for stress relief.
title: Bubble: Breathing Companion

description: A Chrome extension and browser tool that makes documents and web pages more accessible for students with dyspraxia by providing text-to-speech, highlighting, and reading support on iOS and Android browsers.
title: Read & Write for Google

description: A Chrome Add-On that improves writing for students with dyspraxia by suggesting words and phrases as they type, helpful for overcoming motor and spelling difficulties on mobile and desktop.
title: Co:Writer Universal

description: A built-in tool in Microsoft Edge, Word, and Teams that offers customizable reading support with adjustable fonts, spacing, and text-to-speech for students with dyspraxia on iOS and Android devices.
title: Microsoft Immersive Reader

description: Documentation and product page for TD Snap, highlighting the Motor Plan feature as a core component of their AAC software for neurodivergent individuals with complex communication needs.

description: Printable page set featuring 66 Picture Communication Symbols designed for motor planning-based AAC instruction.
title: Motor Plan Board

description: Seeing AI turns a smartphone into a narrator for students with visual impairments or dyslexia. It reads physical handouts, identifies lab objects, and describes diagrams in real time, supporting motor planning by enabling independent access to visual information without physical barriers.

description: An educational guide and activity directory offering practical strategies, home-based exercises, and professional insights for supporting motor planning (praxis).
